item.title: refine_item_spec JSON extraction fails when agent output has indented or wrapped JSON
item.intent: Ensure spec refinement reliably extracts the JSON from the agent's output regardless of indentation or formatting, so retries with spec refinement don't silently fail.
item.description: In refine_item_spec(), the JSON extraction from the agent's raw output uses: sed -n '/^\{/,/^\}/p'. This only matches a { that starts at column 0, so any indented JSON (e.g. the agent wraps it in a code block with indented content, or the JSON is inside markdown with indentation) will fail to extract. The fallback tries markdown fences but the primary extraction is unreliable. The fix should robustly extract a JSON object that contains both .steps and .acceptanceCriteria keys, using jq itself to validate and extract rather than relying on sed line patterns.
steps: Locate refine_item_spec() in .aishore/aishore, specifically the JSON extraction block that uses sed -n '/^\{/,/^\}/p'
Replace the fragile sed-based extraction with a jq-based approach: strip markdown fences first, then use jq to find a valid JSON object containing .steps and .acceptanceCriteria
Use: json_output=$(echo "$raw_output" | sed '/^```/d' | jq -s 'first(.[] | objects | select(.steps and .acceptanceCriteria))' 2>/dev/null) as the primary extraction
Keep the existing validation check: if ! echo "$json_output" | jq -e '.steps and .acceptanceCriteria' — but only if json_output is non-empty
Log a clear error if extraction still fails after the improved approach
Test: verify refinement works when agent wraps JSON in a markdown code block (common output format)
acceptanceCriteria: refine_item_spec succeeds when the agent returns JSON indented inside a markdown code block
refine_item_spec succeeds when the agent returns JSON at top-level with no indentation
refine_item_spec logs 'Refinement produced no valid JSON' and returns 1 when the agent returns no JSON object
No regression in the existing fallback path for markdown-fenced JSON
